name: Tea and Cassia Bark Smoked Chicken
recipeInstructions:
------------------------
Remove the peel from fresh oranges and slice into finger lengths. Allow to dry on a counter until curled.
Smash scallion lengths and ginger coins to release juices. Arrange over the chicken and in the cavity.
Steam for 30 minutes over medium-high heat. Let rest an additional 5 minutes before removing lid.
Drain steaming juices. Remove scallions and ginger.
Line old wok with heavy-duty tin foil. Combine tea, sugar, rice, peppercorns, anise, cassia bark, and dried orange peel in the bottom of the wok. Place chicken breast side up on oiled smoking rack. Turn heat to high until smoke sends up thick plumes, then cover wok and crimp foil shut.
Smoke for 12 minutes, then turn off heat and let rest in sealed wok for 7 minutes.
Remove bird, brush with sesame oil, chop into pieces with a cleaver. Garnish smoked chicken with Hunan Red Onion Pickles and Ma-La Cucumber Fans as side dish.
Garnish:
Remove onion tips and peels. Cut evenly into rings that are 3/8-inch thick. Separate rings, removing inner membranes.
Combine vinegar, sugar, chili flakes, peppercorns, and garlic in a heavy, non-aluminum saucepan. Stir to dissolve sugar, simmer for three minutes. Taste and adjust with remaining sugar if needed.
Add onion rings and toss until wilted and liquid regains simmer. Scrape into shallow bowl and refrigerate before serving. Remove loose membranes and most peppercorns.
